old_claim.note: Basic Shelter is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim_absent: No generic shelter edge remains because the node is now an evidence-locked Roman leather-panel artifact rather than a universal shelter-development stage.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract identifies eight panels from one Roman military tent in Vindolanda's first occupation and analyzes their hide species.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The revised node is a specific assemblage of manufactured Roman tent panels, not an abstract step after all earlier shelters.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Generic shelter chronology adds no technical cause beyond the directly required leather process and would broaden the artifact scope again.

ontology_before: A hypothetical prehistoric hide frame inherited a broad shelter-history edge.
ontology_after: The Vindolanda node is limited to its directly analyzed Roman tent components.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: hide_tent_frames no longer directly depends on basic_shelter.
would_reject_if: The node were broadened again to represent the historical evolution of all portable shelters.
short_reason: A specific Roman artifact does not need a generic shelter-history edge.
